channel.title: David Anderle Writing
channel.description: Writing by David Anderle on systems, risk, and financial systems.

item.title: Why systems fail under stress
item.description: David Anderle on why systems fail under stress, from financial systems to organizations and engineered environments.

item.title: Decision-making when uncertainty spikes
item.description: David Anderle on decision-making under uncertainty, information quality, and better structure when pressure rises.

item.title: What real-world organizations taught me about risk
item.description: David Anderle on what student governance, sponsorship outreach, and finance-facing roles taught him about risk and execution.

item.title: Applying systems thinking to financial risk
item.description: An article by David Anderle on why isolated metrics miss financial risk once dependencies and incentives interact.

item.title: Building resilient financial systems
item.description: An article by David Anderle on resilience, redundancy, and decision design in financial systems.

item.title: Network models of systemic risk
item.description: An article by David Anderle on how network views help explain systemic financial fragility.

item.title: Systemic financial risk modeling
item.description: A flagship project concept for David Anderle focused on network contagion, stress propagation, and systemic financial fragility.

item.title: Climate-resilient grid planning
item.description: A project concept focused on grid reliability, climate stress, and robust infrastructure decisions under uncertainty.

item.title: Global supply chain resilience
item.description: A project concept focused on networked supply chains, disruption scenarios, and robust decision support.
